The liver plays a crucial role in the muscular system by providing essential nutrients and supporting muscle function. It [processes] nutrients from food into forms that muscles can use. The liver [stores] energy in the form of glycogen, which is vital for muscle activity. It also [produces] enzymes that aid in muscle contraction. The liver [regulates] blood flow to muscles, ensuring they receive the right amount of oxygen and nutrients. It [filters] toxins from the blood, which helps maintain muscle health. The liver [synthesizes] proteins that are necessary for muscle repair and growth. It [supports] muscle function by supplying the body with amino acids. The liver [supports] the overall health of the muscular system by maintaining proper levels of hormones and electrolytes. The liver [works] closely with the muscular system to ensure efficient energy production and waste removal.
